chunks.text: Page 1 of 2 ORDER SHEET HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P No.S-148 of 2026. DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE For orders on office objections. For hearing of main case. 18.03.2026. Mr. Ali Ghulam Bhanbhro, Advocate for Petitioner. Mr. Muhammad Ismail Bhutto, Additional Advocate General, Sindh. Mr. Siraj Ahmed Bijarani, Assistant Prosecutor General for the State alongwith DSP Inayat Ali Qureshi (SDPO Hussainabad), Inspector Niaz Ahmed (SHO PS B-Section, Latifabad), Inspector Mirzo Khan (SSP Office, Hyderabad) and SIP Faheem Hussain (DIGP Office, Hyderabad). ***************************************** Mr. Abdul Qadeer Mirjat, Advocate files Vakalatnama on behalf of Respondent No.6, which is taken on record. In compliance with the order dated 06.03.2026, the reports/comments, along with the professional service career record of Respondent No.4, SHO P.S. B-Section, Latifabad, Hyderabad (Inspector Niaz Ahmed Panhwar), have been submitted. The crux of the petition is that, at the behest of the private respondents, the police of P.S. B-Section, Latifabad, Hyderabad, including Respondent No.4 (SHO), are allegedly harassing the petitioner. It is further alleged that a police mobile has been parked at the subject property, notwithstanding the fact that a civil dispute concerning the said property is already pending between the petitioner and the private respondents before a competent forum. Today, the SHO, P.S. B-Section, Latifabad, Hyderabad, is present in Court and has undertaken that the police shall not interfere in the subject matter. Such undertaking has also been submitted in writing. Page 2 of 2 In view of the above, the instant petition stands disposed of, along with pending application(s), if any.

Such undertaking has also been submitted in writing. Page 2 of 2 In view of the above, the instant petition stands disposed of, along with pending application(s), if any. The parties are, however, at liberty to approach the competent forum with regard to the civil dispute, strictly in accordance with law. It is further directed that no police personnel shall interfere in the matter unless so directed by a competent Court of law. JUDGE Ali.
